colors.name: Azul Profundo
colors.cinematographic_notes: Associated with night exteriors, urban alienation, moral ambiguity, and psychological depth

colors.name: Verde Esperanza
colors.cinematographic_notes: Lush natural settings, memory sequences, idealized past, organic textures

colors.name: Ámbar Dorado
colors.cinematographic_notes: Golden hour lighting, opulent interiors, historical grandeur, heightened beauty

colors.name: Rojo Pasión
colors.cinematographic_notes: Blood, violence, desire, warning, heat, aggression

colors.name: Violeta Onírico
colors.cinematographic_notes: Dream sequences, altered states, ambiguous reality, artificial lighting

colors.name: Turquesa Retro
colors.cinematographic_notes: 1950s-70s aesthetics, artificial color grading, retro design, heightened reality

colors.name: Rosa Melancólico
colors.cinematographic_notes: Feminine perspective, emotional vulnerability, soft focus, fading beauty

colors.name: Naranja Vibrante
colors.cinematographic_notes: Kinetic energy, desert landscapes, explosions, sunsets, heat

colors.name: Gris Industrial
colors.cinematographic_notes: Concrete, steel, digital environments, corporate spaces, futuristic bleakness

colors.name: Sepia Nostálgico
colors.cinematographic_notes: Historical distance, memory representation, aged photography aesthetic, dusty landscapes

colors.name: Cian Clínico
colors.cinematographic_notes: Hospitals, labs, morgues, fluorescent lighting, digital displays, alienation

colors.name: Verde Ácido

monochromatic_modes.name: Claroscuro Dramático
monochromatic_modes.description: High-contrast black and white with dramatic shadows and sculptural lighting
monochromatic_modes.cinematographic_notes: Deep blacks, harsh shadows, chiaroscuro lighting, expressionist influence, sculptural faces
monochromatic_modes.historical_context: Film noir, German expressionism, high-contrast photography

monochromatic_modes.name: Monocromático Íntimo
monochromatic_modes.description: Soft, naturalistic black and white with gentle gradations and organic texture
monochromatic_modes.cinematographic_notes: Soft grays, gentle lighting, natural textures, documentary realism, emotional proximity
monochromatic_modes.historical_context: Italian neorealism, French New Wave, naturalistic photography

primary_color.definition: The dominant color identity that defines the film's visual character
criteria: Most frequently occurring color in frame analysis
Color that best represents the film's emotional tone
Color explicitly controlled by cinematographer/production designer

secondary_colors.definition: Supporting colors that contribute to the film's palette (maximum 3)
criteria: Deliberate chromatic relationship to primary color
Film shot in black and white (not colorized)
assignment_logic: If high contrast and dramatic lighting → claroscuro_dramatico

color_shift_films.description: Films that shift color palettes dramatically (e.g., Wizard of Oz, Pleasantville)
color_shift_films.handling: Assign primary color to dominant portion of runtime; note shift in metadata

desaturated_films.description: Color films with very low saturation approaching monochrome
desaturated_films.handling: Treat as color film if saturation > 10%; assign based on hue bias

multi_segment_films.description: Anthology films or films with distinct visual chapters
multi_segment_films.handling: Assign primary color to most narratively significant segment
